title: Autonomous Fleet — run-archive manifest
description: Manifest for a single run-archive directory (.fleet/runs/<run_id>/manifest.json). One entry per first-class artifact the run produced (findings JSONs, verifier summaries, blind-fix files, prompts, responses, diffs, readiness doc, progress doc). The manifest is the audit trail: post-hoc, the entire run can be reconstructed from the listed files + their checksums. See engine-recovery.md ARCHIVE_ENABLED HARD RULES (layout, manifest, mtime ordering, archive_enabled precondition).

schema_version.description: Pinned to 1.0 for this release. The lib's drift-detection test asserts the lib and schema agree on this version. Bumps require synchronous lib + verifier + doc updates.

run_id.description: Deterministic run-id: YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ-<mission>-<6-hex>. UTC timestamp gives sort-by-time auditability; mission slug gives greppability; 6-hex hash disambiguates concurrent runs. Freeform run-ids (operator pet names, branch names) are REJECTED — the regex is intentionally strict because the post-hoc replay machinery indexes runs by this format.

mission.description: Mission slug. MUST match the slug embedded in run_id. The lib validator asserts this cross-reference.

coordinator.description: Optional. The coordinator slug that orchestrated the run (e.g. 'codex', 'claude-code'). Used by INFLATION POST-MORTEM to chain coordinator-specific failure patterns across runs.

base_branch.description: Optional. The branch the run was based on. Useful for replay and for chasing 'why did this run produce different output' divergences.

created_utc.description: ISO 8601 UTC creation timestamp. MUST be earlier than the mtime of any file listed in `files` — manifest is created at the start of the run, files accrete during it, and the validator pins this ordering.

files.description: Every first-class artifact the run produced. Empty arrays are forbidden: a run that produced zero artifacts has no archive, hence no manifest. The `engine-recovery.md` archive_enabled precondition is gated on `len(files) > 0`.

notes.description: Optional freeform operator note. NOT used by the validator; for human readers only. Keep short — the manifest is machine-first.

path.description: Path RELATIVE to the archive directory. MUST NOT contain `..` segments or absolute paths — the manifest indexes a directory, not the wider filesystem. The lib validator rejects path-escape attempts.

kind.description: Categorical kind. Drives the mtime-ordering invariants (see engine-recovery.md ARCHIVE_ENABLED): blind_fix < findings, verify_summary > findings, readiness has the latest mtime. `other` exists as an escape hatch but does NOT participate in ordering checks — a kind=other file with mtime claims is a smell.

sha256.description: Hex sha256 of the file's on-disk contents at archive time. The validator re-hashes and rejects mismatches — without this, the manifest is a list of file names with no integrity property.

mtime_utc.description: ISO 8601 UTC mtime AT THE MOMENT the manifest was written. Used for the kind-ordering invariants. Operators who touch files after manifest emission break the audit trail — the validator detects (file mtime != manifest mtime) and reports it as drift.

producer.description: Worker/reviewer slug that produced this file (e.g. 'p0-reviewer-claude', 'skeptic-pass-gpt5', 'fix-builder-codex'). Used by anti-anchoring's blind-fix-per-reviewer audit (Commit 3) — the validator pairs blind_fix and findings entries by producer to enforce mtime ordering per reviewer, not globally.

bytes.description: File size in bytes at archive time. Cheap drift signal: a manifest entry whose on-disk size doesn't match `bytes` is corruption or mid-flight write. Validator checks size before sha256 (cheap fail-fast).
